title: Sunday chicken with stuffing – what is a Sunday without chicken

seo.title: Sunday chicken with stuffing - what is a Sunday without chicken - Marinda Kook

html:
I know some people don't think chicken is meat. Our family, luckily, is very fond of browned chicken. The stuffing just gives it that extra oomph to make it an iconic dish. I roasted 2 chickens. Tomorrow is another day and a bit of cold chicken or chicken and mayo sandwich sure hits the spot.

I use oven bags to gently cook my chicken to perfection.

YOU'LL NEED:

  • 2 chickens
  • 1 onion
  • 3 cloves garlic
  • 125 ml parsley
  • 60 ml thyme
  • 125 ml breadcrumbs
  • 4 pork sausages (pork bangers)
  • salt and pepper to taste
  • 100 ml flour
  • 100 ml butter

NOW:

  1. wash the chickens under running water and pat them dry with a cloth
  2. in a food processor, chop the onion, garlic, parsley and thyme just until fine – not mushy
  3. squeeze the sausages out of their casings into the onion mixture – give the casings to the dog
  4. mix the breadcrumbs into the sausage and onion mixture.
  5. loosen the skin of the chicken and press the stuffing in under the skin
  6. if there is stuffing left over, press it into the cavity of the chicken
  7. shake the flour into the two oven bags
  8. rub the chickens with the butter and season the chicken with salt and pepper
  9. place them in the oven bags, close the bags and prick about 4 small holes in the bags
  10. place in a roasting pan and roast in the oven at 180 degrees Celsius for 60 minutes
  11. snip the bag open and grill under the element for another 10 minutes or so – watch carefully here so it doesn't burn.
  12. place the chickens on a nice serving platter and thicken the sauce that formed in the bags with Bisto if it isn't thick enough

Chicken reigns supreme in our house.
